And when you look at competitors, it was actually the other side.
So most of their traffic was non-branded terms.

We sat down.
We

lot Oh,

And then right after they joined this eight week accelerated onboarding program that we call the strategy spreads.
And in those eight weeks, it's up to us and our team to really understand what the client cares about, what their goals are, and, um, us to become experts on their, their business.
And in those eight weeks, really the front loaded first three weeks, we're building out all these artifacts, these, um, documents about their business.

think like products and services document, um, personas document, like going really, really deep, ingesting all the materials the company sends us, but also doing our own research and building our own because those building blocks are what inform our workflows and like allow us eventually to scale.
The content, because you're like creating these really narrow, but really deep documents.
It's like 10 steps of like really, really, really important work that can then create like a really good article.
And then we calibrate back and forth with the client on like style, voice, tone.
We create writing guidelines as well.
And so that's like the process of how we get to calibrating an article and like actually creating a first piece of content.
So there's a lot of back and forth with the client.
But we have eight weeks in that strategy sprint process to get them to a hell yes.
We want to continue to work with you because they have a mutual opt out after eight weeks to convert or not.
And so to your point, yes, progress is slow for SEO.
And so what we can do and what we've been trying to emphasize more is like from the start, our goal in these eight weeks is like to get to early signals as fast as possible.
And to.
Unblock everything along the way and to set our system up for success and scale because we're not going to be like word babysitters.
We're training this system that's going to allow you as a marketing team, as a company, to scale up these content efforts, not linearly with headcount, but like explosive scale once we do it, right?

And so the early signals that we're pushing for in the sprints and like the only thing we can really report on when you get like, you know, 15 pieces of content out in the last couple of weeks, which is like the cadence that we try to get to is are these articles getting indexed?
If they are cool, what impressions are we seeing?
And then maybe we start getting some clicks.

And, like, really highlight the early wins and, like, all the little, you know, chef's kiss touches that we can do in that process to, like, get them closer to a hell yes.

so now for every client in the sprints, we set them up in a custom workspace where we track all the prompts that they care about, but we also create custom prompts, which are the questions that people are asking around the articles that we're creating as well.
